data.title: FirstCash Holdings 10-Q: Revenue $1.05B, EPS $2.43
data.description:
FirstCash Holdings reported first-quarter results showing consolidated revenue of $1,051.7 million and diluted earnings per share of $2.43, reflecting stronger retail and pawn activity and higher margins. The company cited growth across pawn lending, merchandise sales and wholesale scrap jewelry as primary drivers for the revenue increase versus prior-year. Improved operating performance lifted net income to $107.7 million, supporting higher cash generation from operations.

Financial Highlights

  • Total revenue: $1,051.7 million, up from $836.4 million year-over-year.
  • Gross profit: $542.4 million (reported as Net revenue on the statement), versus $424.2 million prior year.
  • Operating income: $145.1 million (reported as income before income taxes), versus $111.2 million prior year.
  • Net income: $107.7 million, versus $83.6 million prior year.
  • Diluted net income per share: $2.43, versus $1.87 prior year.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue drivers: Growth was driven by stronger pawn lending activity, higher merchandise sales and increased point-of-sale volumes, along with wholesale scrap jewelry.
  • Geographic and channel expansion: Pawn receivables and inventories expanded across the U.S., Latin America and the U.K.; AFF shifted some finance volume to off-balance-sheet loans.
  • Same-store sales: Same-store retail sales improved, with U.S. growth of approximately 9% and Latin America increases of 33% (15% on a constant-currency basis), indicating sustained demand for value-priced merchandise.
  • M&A and network growth: Completed the H&T acquisition in August 2025 and added 311 net pawn stores globally; also purchased real estate at 15 locations.
  • Operational momentum: The company highlighted continued brand momentum and store-level performance contributing to higher merchandise turnover and lending volume.

Original SEC Filing: FirstCash Holdings, Inc. [ FCFS ] - 10-Q - Apr. 24, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: X-Energy's Shares Jump in IPO, Delivering Wins to Amazon and Ken Griffin — WSJ
data.description:
By Gregory Zuckerman

Nuclear-energy company X-Energy scrapped a public offering less than three years ago after investors showed little interest.

On Friday, X-Energy's shares soared over 30% in their stock-market debut, giving the company a market valuation close to $12 billion. X-Energy has benefited as artificial intelligence drives demand for new sources of electricity.

Its high-profile backers include Amazon.com and Ken Griffin, the founder of hedge-fund firm Citadel.

Ahead of the IPO, Griffin was set to score paper gains of more than $300 million from a $100 million personal investment he made just a year-and-a-half ago, according to people familiar with the matter .

Investment firm Ares Management is another huge winner, other people familiar with the matter said. The firm was set to make more than four times the over-$160 million it invested in the company, the people said, noting that Ares's co-founder, David Kaplan, and a partner, Allyson Satin, were personally involved.

Founded in 2009 by aerospace veteran Kamal "Kam" Ghaffarian, X-Energy spent years developing industrial-size modular nuclear reactors to produce energy for factories and other industrial facilities. By 2022, the Maryland-based company had a deal to sell reactors to the chemical-company Dow's facility in Seadrift, Texas, but investors worried that its potential market was limited.

In December of that year, Ares invested $30 million in the company, along with two hedge funds, aiming to take the company public. But a merger with Ares Acquisition, a blank check company backed by the Los Angeles firm, was scuttled in October 2023 as investors cooled on special-purpose acquisition companies and became cautious on new issues. After the SPAC fell through, Ares added to investment by providing new financing.

X-Energy's fortunes improved as companies began searching for new sources of energy amid the growth of artificial intelligence. In 2024, Amazon, which has backed nuclear-power projects across the U.S. and is eager to get energy from small, modular reactors, became both an investor and a client. Amazon has said it received a commitment of more than five gigawatts of new nuclear energy from X-Energy.

Griffin made his investment in X-Energy in the fall of 2024. Steve Cohen's Point72, Jane Street, Cathie Wood's ARK Invest and the University of Michigan also made investments, X-Energy has said. They all stand to benefit from Friday's IPO. Amazon owns close to 20% of X-Energy.

The investors can't lock in profits until they sell their shares. And it isn't clear how the stock will fare on the public market. X-Energy is still years away from completing a reactor and obtaining regulatory licensing. It has competitors racing to provide nuclear power, including TerraPower, backed by Bill Gates, Oklo and NuScale Power.

Hyperscalers including Amazon, Microsoft and Meta have a growing demand for electricity to power massive data centers and manage AI processing, sparking enthusiasm for nuclear power.

Write to Gregory Zuckerman at Gregory.Zuckerman@wsj.com

data.title: 'Exciting New Role' for Wall Street Research? Checking AI's Work — WSJ
data.description:
By Ed Ballard

Few supply chains are more obscure, yet more fundamental to the economy, than the nexus of hydrocarbons, sulfur and fertilizers.

Insight into this arcane market is in demand because the effective closure of the Strait of Hormuz has throttled the export of sulfur and phosphates from the Middle East. Analysts at brokerage firm Panmure Liberum say an investor sent them a research report on the economic ripples of this supply-chain crunch. The report had been written by an AI-and the investor wanted them to fact-check it.

"Lack of sulfur specialists out there forces these investors to engage AI for answers => but then, they're not sure if the AI answers are reasonable/correct => so, they send the AI dump to sell-side analysts, to see what they think, etc.," wrote Panmure Liberum analysts including Tom Price. They called this vetting an "exciting new role for sell-side research."

So, how reliable was the AI-generated report? It got much of the basics right, but the analysts disputed the report's most striking conclusion-that a shortage of sulfuric acid "threatens the supply chain for consumer electronics and military-grade avionics."

In fact, these high-end industries only need relatively small amounts of acid, and they should be able to pay up for the available supplies. "Nope, not convinced," the humans conclude.

This item is part of a Wall Street Journal live coverage event. The full stream can be found by searching P/WSJL (WSJ Live Coverage).

data.title: P&G Reports 3% Organic Growth, Flags Potential $1 Billion Oil-Driven Cost Hit
data.description:
Procter & Gamble is showing signs of steady execution, but emerging cost pressures tied to energy markets could start to shape the next phase of its outlook. In its fiscal third quarter ended March 31, the company delivered 3% organic sales growth, exceeding even the most optimistic expectations and marking its strongest performance in more than a year. Volume trends also came in ahead of forecasts, suggesting that recent efforts to refine product formulations and position offerings as more effective than competitors may be gaining traction. Chief Executive Officer Shailesh Jejurikar highlighted broad-based growth across categories, with the company continuing to step up investment behind innovation and brand positioning.

At the same time, the cost environment is becoming more complex. Shares moved higher following the release, rising as much as 4.6%, but management pointed to rising commodity exposure as a developing headwind. The company now expects about $150 million in after-tax commodity cost impacts for the current fiscal year, compared with prior expectations for a neutral effect, while maintaining its outlook for roughly $400 million in tariff-related expenses. Transportation costs are already contributing to pressure in the near term, with gross margin declining to 49.5% from 51% a year earlier. Given that a meaningful portion of its products are petroleum-based, Procter & Gamble remains sensitive to sustained increases in oil prices.

Looking ahead, that exposure could become more pronounced if energy prices remain elevated. Management indicated that if oil stays above $100 per barrel for an extended period, the company could face an additional $1 billion after-tax impact in fiscal 2027, which begins in July. To offset this, Procter & Gamble may lean on productivity initiatives, supplier adjustments, and product reformulations, potentially combined with selective pricing actions alongside continued innovation. For now, the US consumer appears stable, though there are signs of more value-focused behavior, including a shift toward smaller pack sizes and promotional activity. How these dynamics evolve could play a key role in determining whether the company can sustain its recent volume momentum while managing rising input costs.

data.title: Low Volume Seen in CBOT Grain Futures — Market Talk
data.description: Volumes being traded on the CBOT are seen as low, keeping trading mostly listless as of Friday afternoon, says AgResource in a note. "Interest in trading is dull with rallies capped by favorable Central U.S. weather with a forecast improvement for Plains rainfall," says the firm. Indications of the peace process in the Middle East conflict moving forward is giving some areas of the market hope of a reopened Strait of Hormuz and mitigated supply-chain and economic damages. Grains are not seeing much stimulation from the headlines though, with trader focus instead being on how weather in the U.S. unfolds heading into May. CBOT corn is down 0.2%, soybeans are up 0.3%, and wheat down 0.8%. (kirk.maltais@wsj.com)

data.title: Lockheed Martin Seen With Risk From Classified Program Spending — Market Talk
data.description: Lockheed Martin's cash is at risk from increased spending on classified programs, Bank of America analysts write in a note, cutting their price target to $600. Classified programs are expected to burn through between $500 million and $700 million, with capital expenditures rising to $2.7 billion by the end of the year. This translates to lower cash flow, they write, cutting their free-cash-flow estimate to $6.49 billion at the end of this year, from $6.7 billion. "We reiterate our Neutral rating given the upside opportunity is well understood, but we see the stock being held back by lack of visibility on classified program and lumpiness between now and the anticipated munitions ramp up," the analysts write. Lockheed shares slide 4.4% (elias.schisgall@wsj.com)

data.title: 3BL Content Editor: A Guide to Formatting, Media & HTML Specifications
data.description:
This guide provides a comprehensive overview of how the editor works, what it supports, and how to maximize performance and discoverability using structured content.

NORTHAMPTON, MA / ACCESS Newswire / April 24, 2026 / 3BL Content Editor: Formatting, Media & HTML Specifications

The 3BL Editor is a structured, HTML-based publishing environment. Formatting is not decorative - it is a technical decision that affects how content is rendered, indexed, and distributed. This guide provides a comprehensive overview of how the editor works, what it supports, and how to maximize performance and discoverability using structured content.

Character Limits

Every field in the editor has a defined limit that affects how your content previews across channels - from email inboxes to aggregator feeds. These aren't soft guidelines; exceeding them causes truncation downstream.

Field

Limit

Notes

Headline

255 characters

Target 60 for search display

Subheadline

255 characters

Doubles as SEO meta description

Body

No limit

Full article content

Short teaser

280 characters

Used in email distribution previews

Writing a headline under 60 characters isn't just an SEO best practice - it's the threshold at which most search engines display the full title without truncation. The 255-character field gives you flexibility, but 60 is the practical target.1

Supported HTML Elements

Text Structure & Semantics

Well-structured content starts with the right tags. Headings, paragraphs, and text formatting elements do more than control appearance - they signal hierarchy to the systems that distribute and index your content.

  • Bold signals importance to both readers and search systems.

  • Italic works well for titles or technical terms being introduced.

  • Underline is supported but use sparingly to avoid confusion with links.

  • Superscript and subscript render correctly for use cases like COCO or trademark symbolsTM — both travel cleanly through distribution.

Lists

When sequence matters, use an ordered list:

  • Lead with your most important claim in the headline and H1

  • Support it with evidence in modular, self-contained sections

  • Close with a clear takeaway or call to action

  • Keep each section focused on one idea

When information is parallel but not sequential, use bullets:

  • Semantic headings at every major section break

  • Descriptive hyperlink anchor text

  • Alt text on every image

  • Embeds placed within the body, not isolated at the top or bottom

Links

The tag supports href, alt, target, title, and rel attributes. Use descriptive anchor text for both accessibility and search performance. Read more about 3BL's framework for optimizing content in our 2026 LLM and Generative AI Writing Guide.

Content Sanitization & Unsupported Elements

The editor automatically removes unsupported or unsafe elements on save. The most common ones teams run into:

  • Special characters, emojis, and math symbols

  • (except for specific oEmbed use cases)

Formatting that looks correct in the editor can degrade silently on downstream endpoints. A table that renders cleanly on 3BL Media may lose its header row on a wire service. Test every rich element against your full distribution stack before publishing.

Rich Media: Embeds & Images

Video Embeds

oEmbed is supported for YouTube, Vimeo, DailyMotion, and Spotify. Place embeds within the body of the article for the best rendering consistency across endpoints.

Images

Supported formats are PNG and JPEG only, with a maximum file size of 100MB. Every image should include descriptive alt text.

Credit: 3BL Media

Rich Content & Performance Considerations

Rich content affects rendering behavior, how information is consumed by search engines, accessibility, and consistency distributed across channels.

  • Your headline should clearly communicate what the content is about in less than 60 characters.

  • Use the description to add context about why this topic matters and why your organization is positioned to speak about it.

  • The first header (H1) should mirror your headline, using words that communicate authority or nod toward search intent.

  • Secondary headers (H2, H3) help break up your content - more readable to both humans and robots than a long unbroken block of text.

  • Keep each section modular, with one clear idea per section.

  • Add descriptive alt text to images to help visually impaired readers and AI systems interpret the visuals you use.

The 3BL Content Editor gives marketing, communications, and PR teams the creative flexibility to produce rich, multimedia-driven stories - while ensuring content is structured, sanitized, and distributed consistently across 3BL's network of 79 partner sites.

1Based on Google's standard search result title display behavior as of 2026.

Talk to our team.

From the Archive

Find more stories and multimedia from 3BL at 3blmedia.com.

Contact Info:

Website: https://www.3blmedia.com/profiles/3bl

Email: info@3blmedia.com

SOURCE: 3BL

data.title: Bolivia police search offices of state energy firm YPFB
data.description:
Bolivia's anti-corruption police and the public prosecutor’s office on Friday raided the offices of state energy firm YPFB in the cities of La Paz and Santa Cruz as part of a probe into alleged irregularities in the purchase of fuel.

The raid was seen by a Reuters witness and confirmed by officials to local media.

The investigation follows complaints filed by members of Congress and Vice President Edmand Lara, who flagged alleged irregularities in the costs of oil procurement compared to previous administrations.

YPFB did not immediately respond to a request for comment.

The probe is investigating contracts and oil purchases involving the global commodity trading firm Trafigura RIC:TRAFGF.UL . The Bolivian government and the company have been engaged in a spat over alleged fuel smuggling, with the government halting Trifugra's contracts in early April, a move that the company rejected.

The public prosecutor's office has stated that the investigation is moving forward, though no suspects have yet been identified.

Bolivia is grappling with an ongoing fuel crisis, marked by long lines at gas stations, as the center-right government seeks to stabilize public finances after inheriting an economy in turmoil.

The president of YPFB, Claudia Cronenbold, resigned from her post on Wednesday, less than a month after being appointed, stating the company required a long-term restructuring.

The Bolivian government on Thursday appointed Sebastian Daroca as YPFB's new interim president.

data.title: Nasdaq, S&P 500 Rises as Ceasefire Extension Calms Markets
data.description:
U.S. stock indexes showed a split performance Friday as markets reacted to geopolitical developments in the Middle East, including a temporary extension of the Israel-Lebanon ceasefire.

The S&P 500 inched up about 0.2%, while the Nasdaq Composite gained roughly 0.7%, helped by strength in technology shares. The Dow Jones Industrial Average, however, slipped about 0.4%, indicating more cautious positioning in industrial names.

In fixed income markets, Treasury yields were largely steady. The benchmark 10-year yield edged up by one basis point to around 4.34%, while the two-year yield dipped slightly to near 3.84%, pointing to limited shifts in rate expectations.

U.S. President Donald Trump said the truce between Israel and Lebanon would be extended by three weeks following discussions in Washington.

At the same time, reports of additional naval mine activity in the Strait of Hormuz and a firm U.S. stance toward potential threats appeared to keep investors attentive to risks around global energy supply routes.

data.title: Apogee Enterprises 10-K: $1,404.7M Revenue, $2.52 Diluted EPS
data.description:
Apogee Enterprises reported consolidated net sales of $1,404.7 million and diluted earnings per share of $2.52 for the year, with revenue modestly higher year‑over‑year driven by the UW Solutions acquisition while earnings declined due to margin pressure. Gross profit and operating income fell notably, reflecting higher input costs and lower volumes across key businesses. The company cited restructuring actions under Project Fortify to drive future savings and operational improvements.

Financial Highlights

  • Net sales: $1,404.7 million, up 3.2% vs prior year (driven by UW Solutions acquisition and offset by lower volume)
  • Gross profit: $319.5 million, down 11.2% vs prior year; gross margin 22.7% (pressures from higher aluminum costs, lower volume)
  • Operating income: $84.5 million, down 28.5% vs prior year; operating margin 6.0% (SG&A 16.7% of sales)
  • Net earnings: $54.1 million, down 36.4% vs prior year; effective tax rate 30.1%
  • Diluted EPS: $2.52, down from $3.89 prior year (adjusted diluted EPS $3.47 per share)

Business Highlights

  • Revenue mix and segment shifts: Consolidated sales growth was driven by the acquisition of UW Solutions; Performance Surfaces surged ~62% on an inorganic basis while Architectural Glass and Metals experienced double‑digit volume declines.
  • Acquisition impact: UW Solutions expanded Apogee's offering in high‑performance coated substrates and broadened product mix for distribution centers and graphic arts.
  • Operational initiatives: Project Fortify Phase 2 resulted in $27.4 million of charges and is expected to deliver approximately $26 million of annualized pre‑tax savings; actions are substantially completed in FY2026.
  • Backlog and contracting: Architectural Services backlog remained robust at $693.8 million, with about 35% of revenue coming from longer‑term fixed‑price contracts.

Original SEC Filing: APOGEE ENTERPRISES, INC. [ APOG ] - 10-K - Apr. 24, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Defense Stocks Selloff Deepens. The Sector Can Survive a 'Blue Wave.' — Barrons.com
data.description:
Al Root

The selloff in defense stocks has gotten out of hand, from the perspective of Citi analyst John Godyn.

Shares of Lockheed Martin, Northrop Grumman, General Dynamics, RTX, and L3Harris Technologies are down 14% since the Iran war started and 8% since Monday.

And this week's losses are despite Northrop and Lockheed reiterating full-year financial guidance on earnings calls, and RTX raising its outlook for the year.

"In just a few weeks, the top-down narrative on defense stocks has completely flip-flopped," wrote Godyn on Thursday.

That narrative was built on higher military spending in the U.S. and Europe, driving faster sales and earnings growth — justifying share gains and valuation increases. RTX stock, for instance, trades for about 25 times earnings expected over the coming 12 months. A year ago, it traded for closer to 20 times.

"The root cause of the selloff is the simultaneous release of an unusually large defense budget request with a spike in 'blue wave' risks stemming from President Trump's low approval ratings driving 'peak defense' fears," added Godyn.

The blue wave Godyn referred to would be significant gains by Democrats in this year's midterm elections, especially in the House and Senate.

As for U.S. military spending, Trump requested about $1.5 trillion for the Pentagon in fiscal 2027 — a record — and up about 50% from fiscal 2026.

"Where is your upside to a +50% annual DoD budget request, with an unexpected military conflict on top?" asked Vertical Research Partners analyst Rob Stallard in a Tuesday report, after GE Aerospace and RTX earnings. "Similarly in Europe, there continue to be periodic concerns over the impact of 'peace in Ukraine' and what that could do to the regional military spending trajectory..."

Regardless of whether Democrats win big in November, Godyn stands firm that both parties can work out a budget agreement — and that defense manufacturers can thrive,

"Blue Wave or not, we continue to believe that the defense stocks in our coverage that are well-aligned with bipartisan budget priorities can sustain elevated revenue growth rates for some time and that the recent pullback has reached tactical extremes," wrote Godyn.

He rates shares of Northrop, RTX, and L3Harris Buy. He rates Northrop and General Dynamics stocks Hold.

Those ratings align with his peers. More than half of the analysts covering his three Buy-rated names rate shares Buy. Overall, 59% of analysts covering RTX rate its shares Buy. The average Buy-rating ratio for stocks in the S&P 500 typically ranges from 55% to 60%. The Buy-rating ratios for Northrop and L3 are 54% and 68%, respectively.

The Buy-rating ratios for Lockheed and General Dynamics are lower at 33% and 50%, respectively.

The five trade for an average of about 22 times earnings expected over the coming 12 months. A year ago, the average multiple was about 18 times.

While all five stocks are more expensive than a year ago, there still appears to be an opportunity to buy the dip in Wall Street's more popular names.

Write to Al Root at allen.root@dowjones.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.
